title: Level 366
wikitext:
{{DISPLAYTITLE:Level 366: "Maximum Hotel"}}
{{Class Variable|security=Stable|color-h=#FFFFFF|color-s=#FFFFFF|color-l=#FFFFFF}} '''Level 366''' is the 367th level of the Backrooms. ==Description== '''Level 366''', also known as the '''Maximum Hotel''', appears to be a hotel building that is similar to other hotel levels, as it has a distinct color scheme and a contemporary architecture. It has more neon and lights and has been renovated more than the others. The colors change dramatically between areas. '''Level 366''' is divided into five areas each connecting to each other. The gravity and the cuisine at this level is similar to [[The Frontrooms]]. The cycle of day and night which usually has twenty-four hours.Rarely it can last about 20 hours. With entities moving around the level often affect its day and night cycle. During the day, entities typically hide in dark areas, including the hallway which is said to be accessible through wall crevices. There have been reports of entities hiding in large, dimly lit spaces. It is important to not go or near in dimly lit areas, such as wall crevices, or abandoned rooms, due to the entities. Since the entities on this level are often passive during the day, it is generally safe during the day. However, its a caution should be taken around the [[Entity 2|Predatory]] or rarely presumed [[Entity 2|Innocuous Windows]], which are located in areas where windows are built. {{Background|file=The hotel mall.jpg}} The floors are mostly slick, which potentially the wanderer may trip. A strange phenomenon where if the wanderer for trying to explode the level, burn anything, and breaking anything and opening up a wall or anywhere will reveal something [[The Empty|empty]], then sucks wanderer including any wanderer nearby there, in before it self repairs. The level can be self-repairing with anything that can be broken here, even if a wanderer attempts to break the walls, ground, celling, or lights. The air in this level is clean and dust free. The daytime temperature is usually around 20°C or (68°F). But at night, the temperature is usually around 10°C or (50°F). Wind are occasionally reported on the level, though less frequently to ranging from once a week to several months. ==Areas== '''Areas''' may vary depending on the wanderer's location. At this level, areas mainly vary in size, form, and amount of danger. It is important to remember that an area may be small or huge. Which a wanderer was in determines this. ===Main Area=== {{Blurimage|file=Hotel lobby .png|caption=An image of the Main Area.|width=370px}}Wanderers will be noclipped or sent to the '''Main Area''', which is a section of the '''Areas''' in the levels. A wanderer can obtain a key to one of the rooms. In terms of '''areas''', this is the largest area in '''Level 366'''. Additionally, it often serves as a meeting place for wanderers in one location that is larger than the other four. All four of these places are connected by this area. Additionally, this is where the floors and escalator are located. It can reach up to 10 stories. There are neon lights here in a some places of the level. Even though the levels windows are its exclusive feature. Excluding the [[Entity 2|Predatory Windows]] entity. Rarely allegedly seen here is [[Entity 2|Innocuous Windows]], and it may show a forest outside, indicating that the level is set in a forest, albeit it's unclear if this is true or not. Since certain windows during the overnight display the daytime even though its nightfall,Some windows may show a desert or a forest during the winter.Many wanderers have disagreed that this is [[Entity 2|Innocuous Windows]], but still unclear if it's a forest or not as the sun doesn't seem to move. ===Restaurant Area=== {{Blurimage|file=Hotelrestaurant.jpg.jpg|caption=An image of the Resturant Area.|width=370px}}The '''Restaurant Area''' is the third largest area in '''Level 366'''. It appears to be a restaurant, larger than other restaurant levels. Despite its size, this part of the area is slightly smaller than the '''Main Area'''. It remains comnparable, with windows that are illuminating during the day. As the baker or a cashier [[Entity 9|Faceling]], also has almost the same effect from [[Level 11]] effect. This areas cuisine is similar of [[The Frontrooms]], which ecause they offer fast meals in this location, including meat, desserts, and vegetables, and depending on who a wanderer orders from, this fast food may vary. Wanderers may find almond water here, wanderers can get food for free, so it's unclear where all the food where ut came from in [[The Frontrooms]]. Since a wanderer cannot enter the kitchen in this location, and often stopped by [[Entity 9|Facelings]] it is unknown. There is a widespread belief that an animal or allegedly entity in disguise will chop them and cook them, but this has not been confirmed. Rarely, [[Entity 2|Predatory Windows]] are seen in the area, mostly visible on the ceiling.Rarely they can be seen from the walls. Since no entities has ever lurked in this area that often during the night, [[Entity 2|Predatory Windows]] is mainly rare although common '''Main Area'''. '''The Restaurant Area''' is usually safe in daytime. Nighttime is an exception. Since the entities began to wander around and [[Entity 9|Facelings]] became hostile at night. Nighttime is not a safe. ===Meeting Area=== {{Blurimage|file=The Hindquarters.jpg|caption=An image of the Meeting Area of the first picture of a meeting room.|width=370px}}The '''Meeting Area''' is the smallest area. There is only a single small room where a wanderer can meet another wanderer. However, it is by far the smallest '''Area''' because it is only one single room. At night, it is the same as the other two regions/areas; however, because the area in this level is limited, it is hard to survive if a wanderer becomes trapped in a room and entities to run across a wanderer, the likelihood of surviving is low, and the most dangerous area during the night. ===Lounge Area=== {{Blurimage|file=Lounge hotel-airport.jpg|caption=An image of the Lounge Area}} The '''Lounge Area''' seems similar a space with tables and couches. The design is more similar to the '''Restaurant Area'''. However, food is not served in this area. However, since many of the conversation in the area is from wanderers, it is even more than the '''Meeting Area''' or the '''Restaurant Area'''. [[Entity 2|Predatory Windows]] does not seem to appear here. There have been no reports of [[Entity 2|Predatory Windows]]. However, since this area has displayed a landscape perspective, there are unidentified accounts of [[Entity 2|Innocuous Windows]] might be present here. However, it has yet to be proven that its [[Entity 2|Innocuous Windows]] than only a window. There may be infrequent reports of [[Entity 9|Facelings]] entering and leaving the region due to their infrequent appearance. This region has fewer dark areas, and it has been said to be hidden by numerous entities.They mainly hide in tiny spaces often by [[Entity 3|Smilers]] and [[Entity 5|Clumps]]. ===Room Area=== {{Blurimage|file=Hotel hallway.jpg|caption=An image of the hallway in the Room Area.|width=370px}} The '''Room Area''', which is notable for having between 1 and 1000 rooms, is the second largest region/area in '''Level 366'''. The hotel rooms in [[The Frontrooms]] resemble the rooms in the levels. ===Rooms=== {{Blurimage|file=Vhblsi2kcj6a1.jpg|caption=An example inside of the Rooms}}The '''Rooms''' are part of the '''Room Area''' and can be accessed via the keys in '''The Main Area'''. The '''Rooms''' appear to be a bedroom depending on the rooms. Some may include kitchens and bathrooms, or it may include double size rooms, meaning rooms can be double the size and larger; it may include double beds and an extensive kitchen and bathrooms. Depending on the wanderer's current room, this space may be either simple or large. The wanderer must use it to open the right door. However, it is no longer possible to receive from used keys from the wanderers. But the wanderer who escape out of the level, rooms will be reset, and the key is being used once more. Additionally, there are no confirmed cases of a negative floor. [[Entity 2|Predatory or Innocuous Windows]] does not appear or reported in one of the rooms in this area, making it safe. Wanderers can find any rooms before the blackout happens during the night. The rooms on the level are protective, and the lights work on this level. It is where a wanderer may sleep during the night. Going far in one of the rooms, reaching up to 100 rooms, a wanderer will see an elevator. Or use stairs to enter the floor beyond floor 2. ===Elevator=== One kind of '''Room Area''' that is comparable to the elevator in [[The Frontrooms]] is the '''Elevator'''. But from button 1, there are only ten buttons available. They are: *Button 1: Rooms 1–100 *Button 2: rooms 101–200 *Button 3: Rooms 201-300 *Button 4: rooms 301–400 *Button 5: rooms 401–500 *Button 6: rooms 501–600 *Button 7: rooms 601–700 *Button 8: rooms 701–800 *Button 9: Rooms 801-900 *Button 10: rooms 901–1000 ==Time Of Cycle== '''Time of Cycle''' refers to the 12-hour cycle for the '''Level 366''' period or by the day cycle. The level of danger determines entities, Surroundings. and lighting. When the light went out at night, the entities began to roam. During the day, they mainly hide in places that are dark. ===Daytime=== '''Daytime''' is the first '''Time of Cycle'''; it is often safe and secure and is where wanderers meet. Though [[Entity 2|Innocuous Windows]] presumably to appear at the '''Lounge area''' in this level, [[Entity 2|Predatory Windows]] is located in the '''Main Area''' and is rarely seen at the '''Restaurant Area'''. ===Nighttime=== '''Nighttime''' is its most dangerous, after ten to twenty minutes,Sometimes it occurs as early as five minutes or later up to thirty minutes.all of the light in the other five areas except for wanderers room goes out. The entities begin to roam and appear in all five regions after roughly twenty minutes, except for the rooms from the '''Room Area''' if one shuts them off to prevent an entity from entering. Being far distant of ones rooms on the level makes it hard to survive. Any light source will function, though it is not advised to use a flashlight everywhere as this due to the entities will notice even more increasing the risk. However, its [[Entity 9|Hostile Facelings]] and some entities to see and chase a wanderer without the light source. ==Colonies and Outpost== There is only one outpost here, and it is the only one; there are no colonies. ===Survival Hotel Outpost=== The '''Survival Hotel Outpost''' is an outpost that has managed to survive and teaches wanderers how to do the same. During the night, they are mainly found in '''The Main Area''', '''The Restaurant''', and '''The Lounge Area'''. They spend of their time in their room. Members are located in certain rooms. *Members: between 40 and 180 wanderers. *Rooms: it is estimated that between 117 and 327 rooms are in use. *Disbanded: no members have been dissolved, although there have been reports of five to twenty members passing away. ==Entities== In this level, the roaming entities are [[Entity 3|Smilers]], [[Entity 10|Skin-Stealers]], [[Entity 8|hounds]], and [[Entity 5|Clumps]]. At night, they wander and infest the level, and hide in dark areas during the day, but there are additional ones. ===Windows=== The level contains 2 types of [[Entity 2|Windows]]: [[Entity 2|Predatory Windows]] and [[Entity 2|Innocuous Windows]]. ===Predatory Windows=== '''Predatory Windows''' is generally hostile during the day, but it's unclear if they are hostile at night because, depending on the location a wanderer is in, survival is either impossible or nearly due to entities. This survival rate is why it has never been confirmed. Their effect is quite comparable to that of [[Level 39]]. But there has been more angry, reported from knocking from the windows. Wanderers often get paranoid as a result. Windows are usually located in the '''Main Area''', while they are rarely seen in the '''Restaurant Area'''. ===Innocuous Windows=== '''Innocuous Windows''' has not yet been proven. Unknown reports of '''Innocuous Windows''' in the level do exist, though. The '''Lounge Area''' is where they are uncredible sightings of them in '''The Lounge Area'''. There have been claims that they resemble a landscape, however, these claims have not yet been proven. ===Facelings=== '''Facelings''' are aggressive at night and submissive during the day. The '''Main Area''' is where they are most commonly found. '''Restaurant Area''' and is sporadically observed in the '''Room Area'''. ===Passive State=== During its '''Passive State'''. They are generally passive, with exceptions, and are being attacked. They mainly roam from the '''Main Area''', '''Lounge Area''' and uncommonly '''Meeting Area''', and '''Room Area'''. And being a cashier and a chef in the '''Restaurant Area''', for unknown reasons why they attack this. ===Aggressive State=== Twenty minutes into the night, the light went out, resulting in a blackout. And the '''Facelings''' will become hostile, though not all, due to evidence of cashier and other workers are not affected but usually dont panic in the night. A faceling will commonly chases the wanderer if they spot one, apart from the other entities, this has most reported deaths at night. It is advised to spend the night in a room. Because '''Facelings''' would most frequently wander throughout the '''Room Area'''. ==Entrance and Exits== ===Entrance=== *Rarely entering a yellow door in [[Level 5]] will send one here. *One can take one here by locating a door on [[Level 19]] and [[Level 330]] that says STAFF ONLY. *Finding a fire rated door in [[Level 187]] will lead one here. *There is a slim chance of getting here if one finds a hotel painting in [[Level 29]] with no clipping on it. *Finding a double swing doors out of place in [[Level 684]] will lead one here. this out of place door often disappears once a wanderer enters it causing one to never go back where they came from *There is a rare chance that a door with a glitch in [[Level 21]] will take one here. *There is small possibility of sending one here from a random no-clipping at any hotel level. ===Exits=== *One can enter a random forest level by locating an exit door that appears in the level at random. *One can reach [[Level 777]] by locating a black door that appears on the level at random. This sends the wanderer either the outdoor or the indoor section parts of the level, and the door suddenly closes itself, which one cannot go back where they came from.These black and blue doors often can be found only in the '''Room Area'''. *One can reach [[Level 37]] by locating a blue door that appears on the level at random. The door suddenly closes itself, which one cannot go back where they came from. *No-clipping through the mirror in '''Rooms''' will send you to [[Level 305]]. *The elevator will begin to fall upward if button 8 is pressed repeatedly, causing one to pass out and wake up in [[Level 888]]. *Trying to spam press button 9 the elevator will begin to malfunction, knocking one out. They will wake up in [[Level 998]]. *The elevator will crash down if button 1 is pressed repeatedly, sending one to [[Level 999]] when the doors open. *Trying to spam press button 10 more than 100 times will make the elevator glitch out, knocking one out and sending one to [[The Corrupted Instability]]. *A strange phenomenon where one would [[The Empty|be suck in to emptiness]], if one tries to break anything in the level. {{Footnotes}} {{Redirect|Prev=365|Next=367}}{{Collapse|initial=> Open Author & Licensing Information|final=> Close Author & Licensing Information|body= '''Author:''' [[User:Internalfound|Internalfound]]
